analyst_observations: The skill produces a 72 percentage point uplift in aggregate pass rate (100% vs 28%).
The baseline consistently fails aesthetic assertions (font choice, layout originality, color, CSS variables, animations) while passing functional ones.
The sidebar eval is the only case where baseline scores non-zero on aesthetic assertions — it gets functional requirements right (4/6) but still fails on font and aesthetic POV.
The most discriminating assertion is 'avoids generic fonts' — baseline hits Inter on 2/3 evals, system fonts on the third.
The purple gradient / purple accent pattern appears in 2/3 baseline outputs (landing page hero, pricing page accent), confirming the skill's specific prohibition is warranted.
Assertion 'CSS custom properties' is near-perfect at separating skill from no-skill: 3/3 with_skill pass, 0/3 without_skill pass.
The pricing page shows the largest gap (100% vs 17%) — the layout and aesthetic choices are completely different categories of output.
